Mikasa: But, I want to sort it out differently
Mikasa: On KGS they focus a lot on there kyu player's so dan player's are a bit left out
Mikasa: We had three dan player's drop out in the last 2 months saying they are not getting enough competition
Mikasa: So I plan to try to set up ASR-OGS league similar to the canadian go league
Mikasa: Somethign a bit more serious where it will keep dan players and kyu players interested
Mikasa: And, since there are less OGS player's then KGS player's at the moment i think it will work out a little better hopefully
Mikasa: Well, I plan to get help managing it but for now I am waiting on matburt to finish the tournament system he is working on
Mikasa: That's why it is a good place to start quaigon!
Mikasa: When you step back and look at ASR you notice there are so many player's who are in it but there is very little participation
Mikasa: There are prizes for the ASR league as well but still there is so little participation
Mikasa: But, when you look at the CGA league
Mikasa: And almost every match is finished!
Mikasa: So I think OGS is a great place for me to experiment with some different league set ups
Mikasa: To see what suits people best what entertains people the best
Mikasa: I think prizes are just to look good I do not think you actualyl require them but I plan to give some away even though I do not think they are required
Mikasa: Anyway at the moment I know how I plan to do things on paper of course
Mikasa: I have to actually have the league before I know how it's actually gonna go
Mikasa: Not in real but on kgs
Mikasa: But, I am not the owner of ASR
Mikasa: My boss is called ""climu"" on KGS I got his permission to open ASR here
Mikasa: Other ASR admins were not so enthusiastic about it as I am
Mikasa: So I am the only one working on ASR-OGS
Mikasa: Of course there is matburt as well and climu helps me when he can
